front: Short Story: linguist Dr. Louise Banks deciphers the language Heptapod B after nicknaming two aliens Flapper and Raspberry; explores Sapir-Whorf hypothesis
Short Story: includes diagrams of an air-water boundary to illustrate the principle of least time
Short Story: miner Hillalum from Elam climbs the title structure; universe shaped like a seal cylinder that loops back to the surface
Short Story: a device called the 'Predictor' flashes a light one second before you press its button, proving free will is an illusion
Short Story: an air-powered being dissects its own brain and discovers flowing currents of argon; memories engraved on gold foil
Short Story: Pembleton College implements calliagnosia technology to prevent people from perceiving attractiveness
back: Liking What You See: A Documentary (Ted Chiang)

front: Short Story: character dying in car crash is struck by beam of heavenly light and loves God, but is sent to Hell anyway
back: Hell is the Absence of God (Ted Chiang)
